The following system of size designations and diameters is a guide. Other designations including mixtures of sizes are allowed.

(a) Whole – the whole, intact head, which is  trimmed at the base. Small tender modified leaves may be present or attached to the unit; (b) Split – the whole head, cut vertically into two or more sections which may have attached small, tender,     modified leaves; (c) Floretsi - segments of the head, which may have a portion of the attached, measuring at least 12 mm across the top in the greatest dimension. A maximum tolerance of 20  percent m/m is permitted for units in which the greatest dimension across the floret is more than 5 mm and less than 12 mm. Small, tender modified leaves may be present or attached to the units.

The styles of the product shall be determined by the nature of the surface and the nature of the cross-section.

Green beans or wax beans having distinct varietal differences with regard to shape may be designated as:
(a) Round – pods having a width not greater than 1½ times the thickness.
(b) Flat – pods having a width greater than 1½ times the thickness.

Quick-frozen peas of either type may be presented sized or unsized. If peas are size graded, they shall conform to one of the two following systems of specifications for the size names. Other size ranges and designations may be used and should be labelled accordingly.

(a) Whole spinach – the complete tender/young spinach plant with root removed; (b) Leaf spinach – substantially whole leaves most of which are separated from the root crown with a maximum length of the stem of 10 cm; (c) Cut leaf spinach – parts of leaves of spinach generally larger than 20 mm in the smallest dimension; (d) Chopped spinach  – spinach leaves cut into small pieces ranging from 3 to 10  mm in the largest dimension, but not comminuted to a pulp or puree; (e) Pureed spinach (spinach puree) – spinach finely chopped which passes through a sieve such that the leaf particles are less than 3 mm dimension.

page_spans.text: (a) Whole – The whole, intact ear of corn to which a small part of the stalk may be attached. (b) Trimmed whole – The product obtainable from one whole ear after trimming of both ends. (c) Cut cob – Portions of the whole trimmed ear cut transversely into pieces.

(a) Whole leek – the leek plant with roots and non-tender leaves removed. (b) Leek – parts of the whole leek with a length, corresponding to the longest dimension of the package, but not less than 70 mm. (c) Cut leek – parts of the whole leek, cut perpendicularly to the longitudinal axis, minimum length 30 mm, maximum length 70 mm. (d) Leek rings – parts of the whole leek, cut perpendicularly to the longitudinal axis into slices, not thinner than 10 mm and not thicker than 30 mm. (e) Chopped leek – the whole leek chopped into pieces, such that the original structure is almost entirely lost, resulting in a “unit” generally smaller than 15 mm in size.
(a) Yellow (b) White (c) Other – colour depends on characteristics of the variety.

(a) Spears – the head and adjoining portion of the stem, with or without small tender attached leaves, ranging in length from more than 7 cm to 16 cm. The spears may be split longitudinally. Within each sample unit not more than 20 percent by count fall outside the designated length. (b) Florets – the head and adjoining portion of the stem, with or without small tender attached leaves ranging in length from 15 mm to 80 mm with sufficient attached stem to maintain a compact head. The florets may be split longitudinally. Within each sample unit not more than 20 percent by count fall outside the designated length. (c) Cut spears – spears, which have been cut into portions and which may be irregular in shape. Pieces from 1.5 cm to 5 cm in the longest dimension. Leaf material may be present but shall not exceed 35 percent m/m and head material shall not be less than 15 percent m/m. (d) Chopped – broccoli finely cut into pieces less than 1.5 cm in the longest dimension. Leaf material may be present but shall not exceed 35 percent m/m and head material shall not be less than 15 percent m/m.

(a) Quick-frozen Brussels sprouts may be presented sized or unsized. (b) Whether sized or unsized, the amount of frozen sprouts passing a square holed sieve of 12 mm, shall not exceed 5 percent by number. (c) If quick-frozen Brussels sprouts are presented as size graded, they shall conform to a, size name designation and size parameter in mm should be indicated on the package when measured in the frozen condition. The following system of size designations and diameters is a guide. Other designations including mixtures of sizes are allowed.

(a) Whole – the whole, intact head, which is  trimmed at the base. Small tender modified leaves may be present or attached to the unit; (b) Split – the whole head, cut vertically into two or more sections which may have attached small, tender,     modified leaves; (c) Floretsi - segments of the head, which may have a portion of the attached, measuring at least 12 mm across the top in the greatest dimension. A maximum tolerance of 20  percent m/m is permitted for units in which the greatest dimension across the floret is more than 5 mm and less than 12 mm. Small, tender modified leaves may be present or attached to the units.
The styles of the product shall be determined by the nature of the surface and the nature of the cross-section.

Green beans or wax beans having distinct varietal differences with regard to shape may be designated as:
(a) Round – pods having a width not greater than 1½ times the thickness.
(b) Flat – pods having a width greater than 1½ times the thickness.

Quick-frozen peas of either type may be presented sized or unsized. If peas are size graded, they shall conform to one of the two following systems of specifications for the size names. Other size ranges and designations may be used and should be labelled accordingly.

(a) Whole spinach – the complete tender/young spinach plant with root removed; (b) Leaf spinach – substantially whole leaves most of which are separated from the root crown with a maximum length of the stem of 10 cm; (c) Cut leaf spinach – parts of leaves of spinach generally larger than 20 mm in the smallest dimension; (d) Chopped spinach  – spinach leaves cut into small pieces ranging from 3 to 10  mm in the largest dimension, but not comminuted to a pulp or puree; (e) Pureed spinach (spinach puree) – spinach finely chopped which passes through a sieve such that the leaf particles are less than 3 mm dimension.

sections.title: Classification of “defectives”
sections.text: Any minimum sample unit which fails to comply with the quality requirements, as set out in Sections 1. 2.1, 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.2.1 and 2.2.4 shall be regarded as a “defective”.

sections.title: Lot acceptance
sections.text: A lot will be considered acceptable when the number of “defectives” as defined in Section 2.6 does not exceed the acceptance number (c) for the appropriate sample plan with an AQL of 6.5. In applying the acceptance procedure each “defective”, as indicated in Sections 2.1.1, 2.1.2, 2.2.1 and 2.2.4, is treated individually for the respective characteristics.

sections.title: Sizing
sections.text:
(a) Whole leek and leek, may be presented as sized or unsized; (b) The minimum diameter of whole leak and leek, measured perpendicularly to the axis immediately above the swelling at the neck shall be not less than 10 mm; (c) When sized, the difference between the largest and smallest diameter of the leeks in the same package, measured perpendicularly to the axis immediately above the swelling at the neck, shall be not more than 10 mm.

(a) Quick-frozen whole and cut green beans and wax beans may be presented sized or unsized.
(b) If round type beans are presented as size graded on diameter, they shall conform when measured in the thawed conditions, to the following size designation for the size names. However, other size designations may be used, and should be labelled accordingly.
Table 1: Size designation
Size designations Bean pod diameter in mm measured by passing through parallel bars
 (a) Extra small up to 6.5
 (b) Very small up to 8
 (c) Small up to 9.5
 (d) Medium up to 11
 (e) Large over 11

sections.title: Defects and allowances
sections.text:
If size graded, the product shall contain not less than 80 percent by number of whole leek of the declared size. For tolerances based on the minimum sample unit indicated in Section 2.2.4, visual defects shall be assigned points in accordance with the appropriate tables in this Section. The maximum number of defects permitted is the total allowable points rating indicated for the respective defect categories: minor and major or the combined total of the foregoing categories. Table 1 – Whole leek  Defect Defect categories Minor Major Total (a) Discolouration    (i) Minor 2   (ii) Major  2  (b) Damaged  2  (c) EVM 1   (d) Roots  2  (e) Parts of roots 1   (f) Poorly trimmed  2  (g) Loose leaves 1   Total allowable points 8 6 10 (Sample size 20 pieces) Table 2 – Leek, cut leek, leek rings and chopped leek Defect Defect categories Minor Major Total (a) Discolouration    - Minor 2   - Major  2  (b) Damaged  2  (c) EVM 1   (d) Roots  2  (e) Parts of roots 1   (f) Poorly trimmed  2

For separation and enumeration of visual defects the test sample (standard sample size) is placed in water in a deep tray, and the leaves or leaf portion separated one by one.

sections.title: Styles – “free flowing”
sections.text:
When the product is presented as free flowing, a tolerance of 10 percent m/m shall be allowed for pieces which are stuck together to such an extent that they cannot easily be separated in the frozen state.

(a) When the product is presented as “free  flowing”, a tolerance of 10 percent (m/m) shall be allowed for pieces which are stuck together to such an extent that they cannot easily be separated in the frozen state. When assessing this factor, the sample unit shall be the entire contents of the pack or 1  kg.
(b)  If presented as size graded, the product shall contain not less than 80 percent by number of bean pods of the declared size or smaller sizes. Of the 20  percent by number which may be of larger sizes, not more than a quarter may be of the second size larger and none may be larger than the second size larger.

sections.title: Size designation
sections.text:
The packages shall bear clear directions for keeping from the time they are purchased from the retailer to the time of their use, as well as directions for cooking.

If a term designating the size of the beans is used: (a) it shall be supported by the size in mm as shown in Section 2.4.5.2; and/or (b) the words “extra small”, “very small”, “small”, “medium”, or “large” as appropriate; and/or (c) by a correct graphic representation on the label of the size range to which the beans predominantly conform; and/or (d) the customary method of declaring size in the country in which the product is sold.

Small tender modified leaves may be present or attached to the unit.

sections.title: Nature of the surface
sections.text: The product may be presented in any one of the following styles including: (a) Straight cut – strips of potato with practically parallel sides and with smooth surfaces; or (b) Crinkle cut – strips of potato with practically parallel sides and in which two or more sides have a corrugated surface.

sections.title: Dimensions of the cross-section
sections.text:
The cross-sectional dimensions of strips of quick-frozen French-fried potatoes that have been cut on all four sides (styles (a) and (b) above) shall not be less than 4 mm when measured in the frozen condition. The quick-frozen French-fried potatoes within each pack shall be of similar cross-sectional dimensions. The product may be identified: (a) by the approximate dimensions of the cross-sections or by reference to the following system for designations: Table 1. Size designations Designation Dimension in mm across the largest cut surface (a) Shoestring 4–8 (b) Medium > 8–12 (c) Thick cut > 12–16 (d) Extra large > 16 Uniformity Uniformity may be expressed as a tolerance of 10 percent by length of non-conforming styles units applies, when specific lengths are not indicated.

Table 1 – Specifications for sizing Size designation Round hole sieve size in mm Specification A 1) Small up to 8.75 2) Medium up to 10.2 3) Large over 10.2 Specification B 1) Extra small up to 7.5 2) Very small up to 8.2 3) Small up to 8.75 4) Medium up to 10.2 5) Large over 10.2

sections.title: Definition of visual defects
sections.text: External defects: are blemishes or discolouration (either internally or on the surface) due to exposure to light, mechanical, pathological or pest agents, eye material or peeling remnants.(a) Minor defect – a unit affected by disease, dark or intense discolouration, eye material, or dark peel covering an area or a circle greater than 3 mm but less than 7 mm in diameter; pale brown peel or light discolouration of any area greater than 3 mm in diameter.(b) Major defect – a unit affected by disease, dark or intense discolouration, eye material, or dark peeling covering an area or a circle greater than 7 mm but less than 12 mm in diameter.(c) Serious defect – a unit affected by disease, dark or intense discolouration, eye material, or dark peel covering an area or a circle of 12 mm in diameter or more.Note: “Slight” external defects which in either area or intensity fall below the definition shown for minor defects shall be ignored. Sorting defects(a) Sliver – a very thin unit (generally an edge piece) which will pass through a slot the width of which is 50% of the minimum dimension of the nominal or normal size.(b) Small piece – any unit less than 25 mm in length.(c) Scrap – potato material of irregular form not conforming to the general conformation of French-fried potatoes. Frying defects Burnt pieces – any unit which is dark brown to black and hard due to gross over frying.

sections.title: Types
sections.text: (a) Any suitable variety of peas conforming to species Pisum sativum L may be used. (b) The product shall be presented as “peas” or may be presented as “garden peas” provided they meet the organoleptic and analytical characteristics. (c) Sweet green wrinkled varieties or hybrids having similar characteristics included.

sections.title: Tolerances for sizes
sections.text: If size graded, the product shall have a minimum of 80  percent either by number or weight of peas of the declared size, or of smaller sizes. It shall contain no more than 20 percent either by number or weight of peas of the next two larger adjoining sizes when applicable.

sections.text: For tolerances based on the standard sample sizes indicated in Section 2.2.4, visual defects shall be assigned points in accordance with the appropriate table in this section. sentences.original:
Quick-frozen vegetables are the products:
(1) prepared from substantially sound, fresh (barring mature processed peas) or frozen vegetables, as defined in the corresponding annexes, having reached appropriate maturity for processing.

classification.sentence:
They undergo operations such as washing, peeling, grading, cutting, blanching/deactivation of enzyme activity , etc. depending on the type of product.
(2) made from vegetables which were subjected to a quick -freezing process,ii and maintained at -18 °C or colder at all points in the cold chain, subject to permitted temperature tolerances.
classification.pattern: [They] undergo operations such as [operations] depending on [product]. (2) made from vegetables subjected to [process] and maintained at [temperature] subject to [tolerances].

components.subject: They
components.operations: washing, peeling, grading, cutting, blanching/deactivation of enzyme activity, etc.
components.condition: depending on the type of product
components.temperature: -18 °C or colder at all points in the cold chain

classification.justification: The sentence describes processing steps and conditions descriptively without normative modality.

sentences.original: Quick-frozen vegetable is the product subject to a freezing process in appropriate equipment and complying with the conditions laid down hereafter and in the corresponding annexes.

classification.sentence: 2.2.1 General requirements Quick-frozen leek shall have similar varietal characteristics and be free from objectionable tough parts; and with respect to visual defects or other defects subject to a tolerance, shall be: (a) free from yellow and/or yellowish leaves; (b) reasonably free from damage such as staining, discolouration, or insect injury;  (c) reasonably free from extraneous vegetable material (EVM); (d) reasonably well trimmed; (e) practically free from loose or detached leaves (in whole style only); and (f) practically free from hard parts as “seed heads”.
classification.pattern: [Product] shall have [characteristics] and with respect to [defects], shall be: (a) [requirement a]; (b) [requirement b]; ... (f) [requirement f].

components.characteristics: similar varietal characteristics and be free from objectionable tough parts
components.defects: visual defects or other defects subject to a tolerance

requirements.a: free from yellow and/or yellowish leaves
requirements.b: reasonably free from damage such as staining, discolouration, or insect injury
requirements.c: reasonably free from extraneous vegetable material (EVM)
requirements.e: practically free from loose or detached leaves (in whole style only)
requirements.f: practically free from hard parts as “seed heads”

sentences.original:
It is recommended that during storage, transportation, distribution and retail, the product be handled in accordance with the provisions of the Code of practice for the processing and handling of quick-frozen foods (CXC 8-1976).1

Any minimum sample unit which fails to comply with the quality requirements, as set out in Sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.5 shall be regarded as a “defective”.

Any minimum sample unit which fails to comply with the quality requirements, as set out in Sections 2.2.1, 2.2.2 and 2.2.6 shall be regarded as a “defective”.

Any minimum sample unit , which fails to comply with the quality requirements, as set out in Sections 2.1.1, 2.2.1 and 2.2.4 shall be regarded as a “defective”.

Any standard sample unit, which fails to comply with the quality requirements, as set out in Sections 1.2.1, 2.2.1 and 2.2.4 shall be regarded as a “defective”.

Any standard sample unit, which fails to comply with the quality requirements, as set out in Sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.4 shall be regarded as a “defective”.

Any standard sample unit which fails to comply with the quality requirements, as set out in Sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.4 shall be regarded as a “defective”.

Any sample unit taken shall be regarded as a “defective” for the respective characteristics when it: (a) fails to meet any of the requirements given in Section 2.1; (b) fails to meet any of the general requirements given in Section 2.2.1; and (c) exceeds the tolerances for visual defects in any one or more respective defect categories in Section 2.2.5.

sentences.original:
Any other style in addition to those described in the various annexes should be permitted provided that the product:
(1) is sufficiently distinctive from other forms of presentation laid down in the standard;
(2) meets all relevant requirements of the standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects and any other requirements which are applicable to t hat style which most closely resembles the style or styles intended to be provided for under this provision; and
(3) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

(2) meets all relevant requirements of the standard, including requirements relating to limitations on defects and any other requirements which are applicable to t hat style which most closely resembles the style or styles intended to be provided for under this provision; and
(3) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sentences.original: In addition to the provisions provided for in the corresponding annexes, quick-frozen vegetables shall:  have a reasonably uniform colour characteristic of the variety;  be sound, clean, practically free from sand, grit and other foreign material;  be practically free from pests and damage caused by them; and  have a normal flavour and odour/smell, taking into consideration any added ingredients  as indicated in Section 3.1.

sentences.original:
7.1 It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969),4 the Code of practice for the processing and handling of quick-frozen foods (CXC 8-1976),5 Code of hygienic practice for fresh fruits and vegetables (CXC 53-2003)6 and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.
7.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and guidelines for the establishment and application of microbiological criteria related to foods (CXG 21-1997).7

classification.sentence:
7.1 It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969),4 the Code of practice for the processing and handling of quick-frozen foods (CXC 8-1976),5 Code of hygienic practice for fresh fruits and vegetables (CXC 53-2003)6 and other relevant Codex texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.
7.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and guidelines for the establishment and application of microbiological criteria related to foods (CXG 21-1997).7
classification.function: Recommend preparation and compliance with hygiene and microbiological criteria
classification.pattern: [component: products covered by this standard] be prepared and handled in accordance with [component: hygiene codes and guidelines]; products should comply with [component: microbiological criteria]

components.products covered by this standard: products covered by the provisions of this standard
components.hygiene codes and guidelines: General principles of food hygiene (CXC 1-1969), Code of practice for quick-frozen foods (CXC 8-1976), Code of hygienic practice for fresh fruits and vegetables (CXC 53-2003), other relevant Codex texts
components.microbiological criteria: microbiological criteria established in accordance with Principles and guidelines for microbiological criteria (CXG 21-1997)

classification.justification: The sentence recommends preparation and compliance using 'It is recommended' and 'should comply with', indicating permission and recommendation. No exact template match.

sentences.original:
8.1 Net weight
The weight of the products covered by the provisions of this standard shall be indicated in accordance with the General standard for the labelling of pre-packaged foods (CXS 1-1985).8
When the vegetables are glazed, in conformity with a specific annex, the declaration of net content of the foods shall be exclusive of the glaze.

classification.sentence:
8.1 Net weight
The weight of the products covered by the provisions of this standard shall be indicated in accordance with the General standard for the labelling of pre-packaged foods (CXS 1-1985).8
When the vegetables are glazed, in conformity with a specific annex, the declaration of net content of the foods shall be exclusive of the glaze.

sentences.original:
If a term designating the size of whole leek is used, it shall: (a) be supported by a statement of the predominant range of the maximum diameter of the leek in mm, or fractions of an inch in those countries where the English system is in general use; and/or  (b) conform to the customary method of declaring size in the country of retail sale.

(a) The words “whole kernel” except that the description “whole grain”, “cut”, “sweet” or “kernels” may be used if this is customary in the country of retail sale.

customary method of declaring size in the country of retail sale

sentences.original: External defects: are blemishes or discolouration (either internally or on the surface) due to exposure to light, mechanical, pathological or pest agents, eye material or peeling remnants.(a) Minor defect – a unit affected by disease, dark or intense discolouration, eye material, or dark peel covering an area or a circle greater than 3 mm but less than 7 mm in diameter; pale brown peel or light discolouration of any area greater than 3 mm in diameter.(b) Major defect – a unit affected by disease, dark or intense discolouration, eye material, or dark peeling covering an area or a circle greater than 7 mm but less than 12 mm in diameter.(c) Serious defect – a unit affected by disease, dark or intense discolouration, eye material, or dark peel covering an area or a circle of 12 mm in diameter or more.

classification.sentence: Frying defects Burnt pieces – any unit which is dark brown to black and hard due to gross over frying.
classification.pattern: Noun phrase – descriptive explanation

components.definition: any unit which is dark brown to black and hard due to gross over frying

classification.justification: The sentence defines a term by describing its characteristics; no modality or regulatory function is present.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR QUICK-FROZEN VEGETABLES
pages.summary: Página contém título principal e cabeçalho, sem seções ou texto interno. Adequada para RAG como página de capa com identificação do padrão e versão.
Página com cabeçalho e seção principal '2022 Amendments' em nível 1, com texto explicativo associado. Sem continuidade entre páginas.

A página contém o cabeçalho 'CXS 320-2015  3'. Identificam-se duas seções principais no nível 1: '1. SCOPE' e '2. DESCRIPTION'. A seção '2. DESCRIPTION' é subdividida em várias subseções no nível 2, numeradas de 2.1 a 2.4, com a seção 2.4 possuindo uma subsubseção no nível 3 ('2.4.1 Other styles'). O texto está associado corretamente aos respectivos títulos. Não há indicações de continuidade entre páginas. A estrutura está clara e adequada para uso em RAG.

Na página 4 do documento, o cabeçalho 'CXS 320-2015  4' está presente. A estrutura hierárquica principal inicia na seção 3, que é continuidade da página anterior, e contém várias subseções e subsubseções bem numeradas com seus respectivos textos associados. As seções 4, 5 e 6 também estão presentes, com seus textos e subseções identificadas. Não há indicações de continuação para a próxima página. A estrutura está clara e consistente para uso em RAG.

A página 5 do documento apresenta um cabeçalho repetitivo 'CXS 320-2015  5' e inclui as seções 7, 8 e 9, todas numeradas hierarquicamente. As seções principais (nível 1) são claramente identificadas com seus títulos e números, e suas subseções correspondentes (níveis 2, 3 e 4) são estruturadas conforme a numeração apresentada. O texto relevante de cada seção ou subseção está adequadamente associado, sem incluir cabeçalhos repetitivos. Não há indicações de continuidade de conteúdo entre outras páginas nesta página. A estrutura está organizada e adequada para uso em RAG.

A página apresenta as seções estruturadas de nível 1: 9.3, 10 e 11 com seus respectivos textos. A seção 9.3 é continuação da página anterior, indicada por is_continuation_from_previous_page=true. Não há subtítulos adicionais nesta página. O cabeçalho 'CXS 320-2015  6' foi identificado e excluído do texto das seções para garantir qualidade em RAG.

A página apresenta a seção principal 1 DESCRIPTION com subníveis 1.1 Product definition, 1.2 Presentation (que possui subtítulos 1.2.2 Styles e 1.2.3 Sizing). A seção 1 DESCRIPTION continua da página anterior e continua para a próxima página. O texto na seção 1.2.2 Styles está incompleto nesta página e continua na próxima. Cabeçalho repetitivo foi excluído do texto.

Página contém a seção 2 ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS com subseções 2.1 e 2.2, e seus respectivos subníveis 2.1.1, 2.1.2 e 2.2.1. O texto está associado corretamente aos nós correspondentes. A seção 2 continua da página anterior (is_continuation_from_previous_page=true). Cabeçalho repetitivo foi identificado e excluído do texto.

Página 9 continua a seção 2.2 de um documento técnico, contendo subseções numeradas 2.2.2, 2.2.3 e 2.2.4 cada uma com seus respectivos textos. A página possui cabeçalho identificado, e os textos são frutos da continuidade da seção 2 iniciada em página anterior (is_continuation_from_previous_page=true). A hierarquia está clara sem subdivisões internas para os níveis apresentados.

Esta página contém a continuação da seção 2.2 com a subseção 2.2.5 intitulada 'Defects and allowances'. O texto detalha as tolerâncias e defeitos permitidos, incluindo tabelas, associadas a esta subseção. O título está visível e claramente numerado. A seção já vinha da página anterior, por isso is_continuation_from_previous_page=true. Não há subdivisões internas adicionais nesta página. O cabeçalho 'CXS 320-2015  10' está presente na página para referência.

A página apresenta a continuação das seções 2.3 e novas seções 2.4, 3, e 4 com subseções. A seção 2.3 está em continuidade da página anterior. O cabeçalho da página está presente como 'CXS 320-2015  11'. Todas as seções possuem seus títulos visíveis nesta página, sem continuidade para a próxima página.
